The **2005–06 Serie A** (known as the [Serie A](/wiki/Serie_A "Serie A") [TIM](/wiki/Telecom_Italia_Mobile "Telecom Italia Mobile") for sponsorship reasons) was the 104th season of top-tier Italian [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football"), the 74th in a [round-robin tournament](/wiki/Round-robin_tournament "Round-robin tournament"). The league commenced on 28 August 2005 and finished on 14 May 2006. While [Juventus](/wiki/Juventus_FC "Juventus FC") were originally the first-placed team, this title was put *[sub judice](/wiki/Sub_judice "Sub judice")* due to their involvement in the *[Calciopoli](/wiki/Calciopoli "Calciopoli")* scandal, with [Inter Milan](/wiki/Inter_Milan "Inter Milan") instead declared champions by the [Italian Football Federation](/wiki/Italian_Football_Federation "Italian Football Federation") (FIGC) on 26 July 2006, thus winning the title for the first time in 17 years.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Rule changes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2005%E2%80%9306_Serie_A&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Rule changes")]

Prior to the 2005–06 season, if two or more teams were tied in points for first place, for only one spot in a European tournament, or in the relegation zone, teams would play tie-breaking matches after the season was over to determine which team would be champion, or be awarded a European tournament spot, or be saved or relegated. However, 2005–06 saw the introduction of new rules. If two or more teams ended the season with the same number of points, the ordering was determined by their head-to-head records. If two or more teams had the same total points and head-to-head records, goal difference became the decisive factor.

## Top goalscorers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2005%E2%80%9306_Serie_A&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Top goalscorers")]

The *[Capocannoniere](/wiki/Capocannoniere "Capocannoniere")* (top scorer) of 2005–06 was [Luca Toni](/wiki/Luca_Toni "Luca Toni") of [Fiorentina](/wiki/ACF_Fiorentina "ACF Fiorentina"). His 31 goals was the highest tally since [Antonio Valentín Angelillo](/wiki/Antonio_Valent%C3%ADn_Angelillo "Antonio Valentín Angelillo") scored 33 for [Inter Milan](/wiki/Inter_Milan "Inter Milan") in [1958–59](/wiki/1958%E2%80%9359_Serie_A "1958–59 Serie A").
